data.title: Estimating The Fair Value Of CAR Group Limited (ASX:CAR)
data.description:
Advertisement

Key Insights

  • The projected fair value for CAR Group is AU$30.68 based on 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity
  • With AU$29.70 share price, CAR Group appears to be trading close to its estimated fair value
  • The AU$38.87 analyst price target for CAR is 27% more than our estimate of fair value

Does the January share price for CAR Group Limited (ASX:CAR) reflect what it's really worth? Today, we will estimate the stock's intrinsic value by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting them to today's value. One way to achieve this is by employing the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model. Believe it or not, it's not too difficult to follow, as you'll see from our example!

We generally believe that a company's value is the present value of all of the cash it will generate in the future. However, a DCF is just one valuation metric among many, and it is not without flaws. If you want to learn more about discounted cash flow, the rationale behind this calculation can be read in detail in the Simply Wall St analysis model.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

The Calculation

We're using the 2-stage growth model, which simply means we take in account two stages of company's growth. In the initial period the company may have a higher growth rate and the second stage is usually assumed to have a stable growth rate. In the first stage we need to estimate the cash flows to the business over the next ten years. Where possible we use analyst estimates, but when these aren't available we extrapolate the previous free cash flow (FCF) from the last estimate or reported value. We assume companies with shrinking free cash flow will slow their rate of shrinkage, and that companies with growing free cash flow will see their growth rate slow, over this period. We do this to reflect that growth tends to slow more in the early years than it does in later years.

Generally we assume that a dollar today is more valuable than a dollar in the future, so we discount the value of these future cash flows to their estimated value in today's dollars:

10-year free cash flow (FCF) forecast

2026202720282029203020312032203320342035
Levered FCF (A$, Millions) AU$428.6mAU$471.1mAU$529.8mAU$579.7mAU$619.1mAU$654.7mAU$687.5mAU$718.4mAU$748.1mAU$777.2m
Growth Rate Estimate SourceAnalyst x7Analyst x7Analyst x7Analyst x1Est @ 6.79%Est @ 5.74%Est @ 5.01%Est @ 4.50%Est @ 4.14%Est @ 3.89%
Present Value (A$, Millions) Discounted @ 8.1% AU$396AU$403AU$419AU$424AU$419AU$410AU$398AU$385AU$371AU$356

("Est" = FCF growth rate estimated by Simply Wall St)
Present Value of 10-year Cash Flow (PVCF) = AU$4.0b

The second stage is also known as Terminal Value, this is the business's cash flow after the first stage. The Gordon Growth formula is used to calculate Terminal Value at a future annual growth rate equal to the 5-year average of the 10-year government bond yield of 3.3%. We discount the terminal cash flows to today's value at a cost of equity of 8.1%.

Terminal Value (TV)= FCF2035 × (1 + g) ÷ (r – g) = AU$777m× (1 + 3.3%) ÷ (8.1%– 3.3%) = AU$17b

Present Value of Terminal Value (PVTV)= TV / (1 + r)10= AU$17b÷ ( 1 + 8.1%)10= AU$7.6b

The total value, or equity value, is then the sum of the present value of the future cash flows, which in this case is AU$12b. In the final step we divide the equity value by the number of shares outstanding. Relative to the current share price of AU$29.7, the company appears about fair value at a 3.2% discount to where the stock price trades currently. The assumptions in any calculation have a big impact on the valuation, so it is better to view this as a rough estimate, not precise down to the last cent.

The Assumptions

We would point out that the most important inputs to a discounted cash flow are the discount rate and of course the actual cash flows. If you don't agree with these result, have a go at the calculation yourself and play with the assumptions. The DCF also does not consider the possible cyclicality of an industry, or a company's future capital requirements, so it does not give a full picture of a company's potential performance. Given that we are looking at CAR Group as potential shareholders, the cost of equity is used as the discount rate, rather than the cost of capital (or weighted average cost of capital, WACC) which accounts for debt. In this calculation we've used 8.1%, which is based on a levered beta of 1.145. Beta is a measure of a stock's volatility, compared to the market as a whole. We get our beta from the industry average beta of globally comparable companies, with an imposed limit between 0.8 and 2.0, which is a reasonable range for a stable business.

View our latest analysis for CAR Group

SWOT Analysis for CAR Group

Strength
  • Debt is not viewed as a risk.
    Balance sheet summary for CAR.
Weakness
  • Earnings growth over the past year underperformed the Interactive Media and Services industry.
  • Dividend is low compared to the top 25% of dividend payers in the Interactive Media and Services market.
Opportunity
  • Annual earnings are forecast to grow faster than the Australian market.
  • Current share price is below our estimate of fair value.
Threat
  • Dividends are not covered by earnings.
  • Revenue is forecast to grow slower than 20% per year.
    See CAR's dividend history.

Moving On:

Whilst important, the DCF calculation shouldn't be the only metric you look at when researching a company. DCF models are not the be-all and end-all of investment valuation. Preferably you'd apply different cases and assumptions and see how they would impact the company's valuation. For instance, if the terminal value growth rate is adjusted slightly, it can dramatically alter the overall result. For CAR Group, there are three important items you should further research:

  1. Risks: Take risks, for example - CAR Group has 1 warning sign we think you should be aware of.
  2. Future Earnings: How does CAR's growth rate compare to its peers and the wider market? Dig deeper into the analyst consensus number for the upcoming years by interacting with our free analyst growth expectation chart.
  3. Other High Quality Alternatives: Do you like a good all-rounder? Explore our interactive list of high quality stocks to get an idea of what else is out there you may be missing!

PS. Simply Wall St updates its DCF calculation for every Australian stock every day, so if you want to find the intrinsic value of any other stock just search here.

data.title: Does Ventas’ EPS Surge And Uptrend Signal A Stronger Long-Term Thesis For VTR?
data.description:
• Recent updates indicate that Ventas has been reporting rapid earnings per share expansion, steady revenue growth and rising free cash flow, while consistently exceeding analyst expectations in past quarters.
  • Alongside these fundamentals, Ventas’ shares have been trading in a confirmed upward trend with a tight sideways consolidation pattern that may signal shifting investor positioning.
  • We’ll now look at how Ventas’ rapid earnings per share expansion shapes the company’s investment narrative and future risk-reward profile.

We've found 14 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

What Is Ventas' Investment Narrative?

To own Ventas, you need to believe that its recent return to profitability and improving cash generation can support both its dividend and ongoing reinvestment in healthcare real estate, even with a relatively low forecast return on equity and imperfect earnings quality. The latest earnings beat and guidance lift reinforce the near term catalyst of continued EPS expansion, while the share price’s steady uptrend and tight consolidation suggest the market is starting to price in that story more confidently rather than reacting to a one off headline. At the same time, the news does little to change the core risks: an income stream that has not always translated into fully covered interest and dividends, a valuation that already bakes in meaningful growth, and sensitivity to any disappointment versus those raised expectations.

However, one key cash flow risk behind the dividend story may surprise some investors. Despite retreating, Ventas' shares might still be trading 26% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Five fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community range from about US$33 to a very large outlier, showing how far apart private investors can be on Ventas. Set against that, the recent run of earnings beats and raised guidance keeps expectations high, which could amplify how the market reacts if growth or cash flows slip from here. This mix of dispersed valuations and elevated short term catalysts invites you to weigh several contrasting viewpoints before deciding where you stand.

Explore 5 other fair value estimates on Ventas - why the stock might be worth less than half the current price!

data.title: How Investors May Respond To J.B. Hunt (JBHT) Earnings Beat, Buybacks And Dividend Increase
data.description:
• J.B. Hunt Transport Services recently reported fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results showing higher net income and earnings per share despite slightly lower sales and revenue, while also completing over US$1.00 billion of share repurchases and authorizing a 2.3% increase in its regular quarterly dividend to US$0.45 per share.
  • Together with its adoption of Triumph’s automated payment solution, these moves highlight how J.B. Hunt is using technology, cost discipline, and capital returns to reinforce its role as a major North American transportation provider.
  • We’ll now examine how the earnings beat, supported by cost controls and buybacks, shapes J.B. Hunt’s investment narrative for investors.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 109 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is J.B. Hunt Transport Services' Investment Narrative?

To own J.B. Hunt, you need to believe it can keep turning a flat freight backdrop into steady earnings progress by leveraging scale, technology and disciplined capital allocation. The latest quarter fits that story: revenue was essentially unchanged, but net income and EPS moved higher, helped by cost controls and a very large buyback program now totaling about US$1.00 billion. The new US$0.45 dividend, up 2.3%, reinforces a shareholder‑friendly approach and signals confidence in cash generation, even as the company carries a high debt load. In the near term, the key catalysts remain any firming in freight demand and evidence that efficiency investments like Triumph’s payment platform can support margins. The recent announcements support those themes, but they do not materially change the main risks around leverage, pricing pressure and insider selling.

However, investors should not overlook how that higher debt interacts with a still-weak freight market. J.B. Hunt Transport Services' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 6%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Four Simply Wall St Community fair value estimates for J.B. Hunt span roughly US$153.55 to US$225.25, underscoring how far opinions can spread. Set against recent cost driven EPS growth, dividend increases and heavy buybacks, that dispersion invites you to weigh how much execution risk and freight market uncertainty you think the current price truly reflects.

data.title: Should Loma Negra’s New US$60 Million Bond Issue Reshape How LOMA Investors View Its Balance-Sheet Flexibility?
data.description:
• Loma Negra closed a US$60,000,000 Class 6 bond placement on January 20, 2026, issuing 36‑month bonds at 6.50% interest with semiannual payments to bolster its medium‑term funding.
  • The successful placement highlights continuing credit demand for Loma Negra and reinforces the company’s access to capital markets for future financing flexibility.
  • We will now examine how this US$60,000,000 bond issue reshapes Loma Negra’s investment narrative, particularly around balance-sheet strength and funding optionality.

Find companies with promising cash flow potential yet trading below their fair value.

Advertisement

What Is Loma Negra Compañía Industrial Argentina Sociedad Anónima's Investment Narrative?

To own Loma Negra, you need to be comfortable with a cyclical, Argentina‑focused cement business that has recently seen sales soften and margins compress, with Q3 2025 swinging into a sizeable loss after earlier profit. The big near term questions center on whether earnings can stabilize and how the company manages its cost base in a choppy operating backdrop. Against that, the fresh US$60,000,000 Class 6 bond gives Loma Negra more breathing room, securing medium term funding at 6.50% and reducing immediate refinancing pressure rather than altering the core equity story. It slightly tilts the balance of catalysts toward execution and demand recovery, while the key risks remain weak profitability, volatile trading in the shares, and governance concerns such as limited board independence.

However, one risk around weak recent earnings momentum is easy to miss at first glance. Loma Negra Compañía Industrial Argentina Sociedad Anónima's shares are on the way up, but could they be overextended? Uncover how much higher they are than fair value.

data.title: A Look At Packaging Corporation Of America (PKG) Valuation After Containerboard Price Hike And Share Reaction

See our latest analysis for Packaging Corporation of America.

data.title: Should Mycronic’s New Asian MMX Metrology Order Require Action From Mycronic (OM:MYCR) Investors?
data.description:
• Mycronic AB (publ) recently reported an order from a new Asian customer for its MMX metrology system, valued at US$2,000,000–US$4,000,000, with delivery scheduled in the current quarter.
  • The deal broadens Mycronic’s reach in the Asian semiconductor supply chain and highlights demand for tools serving mature semiconductor nodes and replacing legacy equipment.
  • We will now examine how this new MMX order from Asia shapes Mycronic’s investment narrative, particularly its positioning in mature semiconductor nodes.

Find companies with promising cash flow potential yet trading below their fair value.

Advertisement

What Is Mycronic's Investment Narrative?

For Mycronic, the core investment case still rests on a profitable, high‑margin equipment business with strong positions in mask writing and metrology, priced at a modest discount to peer and DCF fair values. The new MMX order in Asia fits neatly into that story rather than redefining it: at US$2,000,000–US$4,000,000, it looks immaterial to near term earnings, but it does slightly strengthen the short term order momentum ahead of upcoming results and underlines Mycronic’s relevance in mature semiconductor nodes. The more important near term catalysts remain quarterly earnings, dividend decisions, and the broader order pipeline across display and semiconductor customers. Key risks still center on lumpy order timing, dependence on a concentrated product set, and any cyclical pullback in capex.

However, one risk around order lumpiness and visibility is easy to underestimate. Despite retreating, Mycronic's shares might still be trading above their fair value and there could be some more downside. Discover how much.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Three Simply Wall St Community fair values cluster between SEK211.90 and SEK232.58, while the MMX-driven order momentum and ongoing reliance on cyclical equipment spending give very different angles for you to weigh.

data.title: Cathay Pacific Airways (SEHK:293) Valuation Check After Strong Passenger And Cargo Recovery
data.description:
Advertisement

Operating results put Cathay Pacific Airways (SEHK:293) in focus

Cathay Pacific Airways (SEHK:293) has attracted fresh attention after reporting December 2025 and full year traffic figures, with double digit year on year growth in both passenger volumes and cargo tonnage.

See our latest analysis for Cathay Pacific Airways.

The HK$12.58 share price has been supported by an 11.72% 90 day share price return and a 27.65% 1 year total shareholder return, suggesting momentum has been building around the recovery in passenger and cargo activity.

data.title: US Ownership Shift Puts MP Materials At Center Of Rare Earth Supply
data.description:
• The U.S. government is shifting toward direct equity stakes in critical mineral companies, including a significant stake in MP Materials (NYSE:MP).
  • Recent funding and ownership moves involving USA Rare Earth Inc. and MP Materials highlight an effort to secure domestic rare earth supply for national security.
  • This policy direction positions MP Materials as a key beneficiary as the only significant rare earth miner in North America.

MP Materials operates in the rare earths sector, supplying materials that feed into defense, clean energy, and advanced electronics supply chains. With the Department of Defense now a major shareholder, MP is more closely aligned with U.S. policy efforts to build resilient, local critical mineral capacity.

For investors, this tighter link between MP and federal policy could influence factors such as access to capital, long term project planning, and customer relationships. It also raises new questions around how government ownership might affect governance, risk, and potential funding for future stages of MP’s rare earth processing and downstream ambitions.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for MP Materials by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on MP Materials.

Why MP Materials could be great value

The shift toward direct U.S. government equity stakes in rare earth companies signals that Washington now treats these assets more like core defense infrastructure than just another commodity play. For MP Materials, which already counts the Department of Defense as an effective 15% shareholder and operates the only significant rare earth mine in North America, this reinforces its role as a central piece of U.S. supply chain planning rather than a purely commercial operator.

Advertisement

MP Materials narrative, sharpened by "American Sovereign Wealth Fund" backing

This news tends to support the existing narrative that MP is tightly linked to U.S. national security priorities, with the Pentagon previously becoming its largest shareholder through a US$400 million share purchase. Government attention to USA Rare Earth and other peers does not remove MP from the story; it broadens the theme of a domestically anchored, government backed rare earth ecosystem where MP sits as an early and established exposure.

Risks and rewards that stand out for MP Materials

  • Policy support for rare earths, including large government equity stakes in MP and peers, signals sustained interest in building domestic supply chains tied to defense and clean energy.
  • MP has been highlighted as trading at a discount to some analyst fair value estimates and as having earnings growth forecasts, which some investors may view as potential upside if execution and policy support align.
  • Government ownership can come with conditions on capital allocation, pricing or exports, which may limit management flexibility compared with a fully private sector backed miner.
  • There is one flagged risk around insider selling, reminding investors to watch management share activity alongside policy headlines.

What to watch next for MP Materials

From here, it is worth watching how government funding is split between MP, USA Rare Earth and other projects, and whether MP secures additional contracts, processing support or downstream partnerships off the back of this policy shift. You can stay on top of how different investors interpret this by following the latest community views in this narrative hub, especially as new policy announcements and quarterly results come through.

data.title: Should Weakness in Qingci Games Inc.'s (HKG:6633) Stock Be Seen As A Sign That Market Will Correct The Share Price Given Decent Financials?
data.description:
With its stock down 13% over the past week, it is easy to disregard Qingci Games (HKG:6633). But if you pay close attention, you might find that its key financial indicators look quite decent, which could mean that the stock could potentially rise in the long-term given how markets usually reward more resilient long-term fundamentals. Particularly, we will be paying attention to Qingci Games' ROE today.

Return on Equity or ROE is a test of how effectively a company is growing its value and managing investors’ money. In other words, it is a profitability ratio which measures the rate of return on the capital provided by the company's shareholders.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

How Do You Calculate Return On Equity?

Return on equity can be calculated by using the formula:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for Qingci Games is:

4.4% = CN¥82m ÷ CN¥1.9b (Based on the trailing twelve months to June 2025).

The 'return' is the profit over the last twelve months. That means that for every HK$1 worth of shareholders' equity, the company generated HK$0.04 in profit.

See our latest analysis for Qingci Games

What Has ROE Got To Do With Earnings Growth?

We have already established that ROE serves as an efficient profit-generating gauge for a company's future earnings. Depending on how much of these profits the company reinvests or "retains", and how effectively it does so, we are then able to assess a company’s earnings growth potential. Generally speaking, other things being equal, firms with a high return on equity and profit retention, have a higher growth rate than firms that don’t share these attributes.

Qingci Games' Earnings Growth And 4.4% ROE

As you can see, Qingci Games' ROE looks pretty weak. Even compared to the average industry ROE of 9.2%, the company's ROE is quite dismal. However, the moderate 19% net income growth seen by Qingci Games over the past five years is definitely a positive. We reckon that there could be other factors at play here. Such as - high earnings retention or an efficient management in place.

Next, on comparing with the industry net income growth, we found that Qingci Games' reported growth was lower than the industry growth of 25% over the last few years, which is not something we like to see.

Earnings growth is a huge factor in stock valuation. The investor should try to establish if the expected growth or decline in earnings, whichever the case may be, is priced in. This then helps them determine if the stock is placed for a bright or bleak future. If you're wondering about Qingci Games''s valuation, check out this gauge of its price-to-earnings ratio, as compared to its industry.

Is Qingci Games Efficiently Re-investing Its Profits?

Qingci Games doesn't pay any regular dividends, meaning that all of its profits are being reinvested in the business, which explains the fair bit of earnings growth the company has seen.

Conclusion

On the whole, we do feel that Qingci Games has some positive attributes. Namely, its respectable earnings growth, which it achieved due to it retaining most of its profits. However, given the low ROE, investors may not be benefitting from all that reinvestment after all. While we won't completely dismiss the company, what we would do, is try to ascertain how risky the business is to make a more informed decision around the company. To know the 2 risks we have identified for Qingci Games visit our risks dashboard for free.

data.title: Is Abercrombie & Fitch’s (ANF) NFL Fashion Deal the Next Step in Its Brand Reinvention?
data.description:
• Abercrombie & Fitch recently became the first official fashion partner of the NFL and hosted an invite-only, star-studded pre-Super Bowl fashion show, underscoring the league’s push to link sports and style.
  • This collaboration coincides with Abercrombie & Fitch’s strong operating backdrop, including best-in-class margins and very large multi-year earnings growth, amplifying attention on its brand repositioning.

What Is Abercrombie & Fitch's Investment Narrative?

An investor in Abercrombie & Fitch today has to believe the brand’s repositioning can keep resonating with consumers while its current margin profile remains intact. The new NFL fashion partnership fits that story by putting Abercrombie in front of a broader, culturally plugged-in audience, but on its own it is unlikely to move the needle on near-term earnings guidance, which already calls for mid-single-digit sales growth and solid double-digit operating margins. Instead, the deal slightly strengthens the brand and marketing side of the thesis at a time when the share price has pulled back and the company is aggressively repurchasing stock. The bigger swing factors still look like maintaining pricing power, sustaining traffic without heavy discounting, and managing any earnings pressure flagged by forecasts.

However, the same brand heat that supports margins can fade faster than many investors expect. Despite retreating, Abercrombie & Fitch's shares might still be trading 39% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

data.title: Could Diodes’ New Isolation and USB PD Chips Recast Its Edge in Industrial Power? (DIOD)
data.description:
• Earlier in January 2026, Diodes Incorporated launched its API772x RobustISO dual‑channel digital isolators and new AP5378x USB Type‑C Power Delivery controllers, targeting demanding applications from industrial automation and energy systems to high‑power portable devices.
  • The RobustISO isolators stand out by combining reinforced isolation ratings above 12kV with strong electromagnetic immunity and long projected operating lifetimes, positioning Diodes to compete in safety‑critical and noise‑prone end markets.
  • Next, we will examine how this push into higher‑isolation digital interfaces and USB PD3.1 power management could reshape Diodes' investment narrative.

What Is Diodes' Investment Narrative?

For Diodes, you really have to believe in a recovery story built on disciplined execution in niche power and signal products rather than headline-grabbing growth. The API772x RobustISO isolators and new USB PD3.1 controllers fit that thesis neatly, reinforcing Diodes’ push into industrial, energy and higher power portable designs where safety and efficiency matter. In the near term, these launches are more about strengthening the product roadmap and design‑win pipeline than moving the revenue needle, so they probably do not change the key catalysts: signs of margin improvement after a period of compressed profitability and how management allocates capital under the enlarged US$100.00m buyback. The main risk, now sharpened by these introductions, is whether Diodes can win and retain sockets in intensely competitive segments while its return on equity remains low and recent share price performance lags peers.

However, there is a risk that these new products do not translate into stronger profitability. Diodes' share price has been on the slide but might be dropping deeper into value territory. Find out whether it's a bargain at this price.

data.title: XD Inc. (SEHK:2400) Valuation Check After Launch Of Share Buyback Programme
data.description:
XD (SEHK:2400) has started buying back its own shares under a shareholder approved mandate, with capacity to repurchase up to 49,167,523 shares, representing 10% of its issued share capital.

See our latest analysis for XD.

XD’s share repurchase program arrives after a strong run, with a 30 day share price return of 27.89% and a 1 year total shareholder return of 243.35%, suggesting momentum has been building despite some shorter term swings.

If this kind of buyback fueled story has your attention, it could be a good moment to broaden your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With XD trading around HK$82.55 and an indicated intrinsic value gap of about 20%, plus fresh buybacks in motion, you have to ask: is there still mispricing here, or is the market already baking in future growth?

Advertisement

Price-to-Earnings of 26.2x: Is it justified?

XD trades on a P/E of 26.2x, which looks expensive next to both its Hong Kong Entertainment peers and the broader peer group, even after strong recent returns.

The P/E ratio compares the current share price with earnings per share, so a higher number usually reflects stronger earnings expectations or a willingness to pay up for profitability. For a game publisher and platform operator like XD, a richer P/E can sometimes go hand in hand with fast earnings growth and scalable digital economics.

Here, the tension is clear. XD has very strong recent earnings growth and an outstanding 45.2% return on equity. However, the P/E of 26.2x stands well above the Hong Kong Entertainment industry average of 13.6x and the peer average of 10.9x. It is also high compared to the estimated fair P/E of 15.3x, a level the market could move toward if expectations cool or growth normalises.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for XD

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 26.2x (OVERVALUED)

However, you still have to weigh risks such as XD’s P/E compressing toward the 15.3x fair level, or buybacks slowing if earnings momentum fades.

Find out about the key risks to this XD narrative.

Another View: DCF Points to Undervaluation

While the 26.2x P/E suggests XD looks expensive next to its industry and peer averages, our DCF model paints a different picture. With the shares at HK$82.55 versus an estimated fair value of HK$103.43, the model implies XD is trading at a meaningful discount. This raises the question of whether the earnings multiple is too harsh or the cash flow outlook is too generous.

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

data.title: Assessing Travel + Leisure (TNL) Valuation After New Orlando Headquarters And Fortune Recognition
data.description:
Travel + Leisure (TNL) has opened a new global headquarters in Downtown Orlando after investing more than US$36 million, and has also secured a fourth straight appearance on Fortune’s World’s Most Admired Companies list.

See our latest analysis for Travel + Leisure.

These headquarters and reputation wins come after a 90-day share price return of 8.01%. Shorter term share price returns over 7 and 30 days have softened, while the 1-year and 3-year total shareholder returns of 33.80% and 90.92% point to stronger longer run momentum.

If this kind of long term compounding interests you, it may be worth widening your lens to other names and checking out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With Travel + Leisure shares at US$69.83, an indicated intrinsic discount of about 27% and some recent short term weakness, the key question is simple: is there still value on the table, or is future growth already priced in?

With Travel + Leisure trading at $69.83 against a widely followed fair value of about $74.92, the core question is how its recurring, membership driven model supports that gap.

The strong and growing pipeline of predictable, recurring revenue from owner upgrades, management fees, and financing activity (with 75% of revenue recurring), along with a $20 billion ten-year revenue pipeline, underpins dependable free cash flow generation and earnings stability for future periods.

data.title: Assessing Dmall (SEHK:2586) Valuation As Shares Show Strong Recent Momentum
data.description:
Advertisement

Recent performance snapshot

Dmall (SEHK:2586) has drawn attention after a recent 1 day return of 1.65%, building on roughly 3.5% over the past week and about 21% over the past month.

See our latest analysis for Dmall.

Set against this backdrop, Dmall’s year to date share price return of 15.68% and 1 year total shareholder return of 50.35% suggest momentum has picked up recently, even though the 90 day share price return of a 1.93% decline shows that sentiment has not been a straight line.

If Dmall’s recent move has caught your attention, it may be a good moment to broaden your watchlist and look at high growth tech and AI stocks that could fit a similar digital theme.

With Dmall still loss making yet trading at HK$8.63 against an analyst price target of HK$15.09, you have to ask: is this a genuine mispricing, or is the market already factoring in future growth?

Preferred Price-to-Sales Multiple of 3.5x: Is it justified?

Based on the latest data, Dmall trades on a P/S of 3.5x, which screens as mixed value when you line it up against both peers and an estimated fair level.

The P/S ratio compares the company’s market value to its revenue, and it is often used for loss making software names where earnings are not yet a guide. For Dmall, this matters because the business is still loss making, with revenue of CN¥1,998.27m and a reported loss of CN¥1,892.88m.

On one hand, Dmall is flagged as good value versus a peer average P/S of 4.2x. This suggests the market is not assigning the same revenue multiple as similar Hong Kong software companies. On the other hand, the SWS fair ratio framework marks Dmall as expensive versus an estimated fair P/S of 2.8x. This is a level the market could potentially move towards if expectations cool or revenue does not track forecasts.

Against the broader Hong Kong software industry, Dmall is also described as expensive, with its 3.5x P/S above the industry average of 2.7x. This again hints that investors are currently paying a premium per unit of sales relative to many local peers.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for Dmall

Result: Price-to-Sales of 3.5x (OVERVALUED)

However, there are still clear risks, including ongoing CN¥1,892.88m net losses and the possibility that revenue growth of 15.91% does not support a 3.5x P/S multiple.

Find out about the key risks to this Dmall narrative.

Another view using our DCF model

While the 3.5x P/S points to an expensive share price versus the fair ratio of 2.8x, our DCF model is even more cautious. At HK$8.63, Dmall trades above an estimated future cash flow value of HK$3.90, which implies less room for error if the story does not play out as forecast.

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.
